title: KD Platform: ICP-native NOC Dashboard (React + React Query + Actor calls)

requirements.summary: Add a new NOC dashboard route (/kd-platform/noc) with sections for AI, Treasury, DAO, ZK, Omni-mesh, and Universal Launch, using actor-backed React Query data.
acceptanceCriteria: A new page route exists and is reachable from the app router.
The page shows distinct UI sections for AI, Treasury, DAO, ZK, Omni-mesh, and Universal Launch.
All data displayed on the page is loaded via React Query hooks calling the backend actor.
User-facing strings on the new page are written in English.

file_operations.description: Create the NOC dashboard page component that renders distinct cards/sections for AI Status, Treasury Metrics (including a formatted TVL string), DAO Proposals (list + vote buttons), ZK Root display + Generate Solvency Proof action, Omni-mesh status indicator, and a Universal Launch overlay trigger; ensure all user-facing copy is English and all data/actions come from React Query hooks (no REST fetch usage).
Register the new React route at path '/kd-platform/noc' and wire it into the TanStack Router route tree so it is reachable from the app router.

Implement/port a UniversalLaunchOverlay component used by the NOC page; it should read omni-mesh status and universal launch state via React Query hooks and trigger the universal launch action via a mutation hook (no fetch('/api/...')).

requirements.summary: Add NOC React Query hooks in useQueries.ts for AI/Treasury/DAO/ZK/Omni-mesh/Universal Launch actor calls, following existing queryKey and invalidation patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: NOC-related hooks exist in useQueries.ts and compile in TypeScript.
DAO vote mutation invalidates the proposals query so vote totals refresh.
ZK proof generation invalidates/refetches current root so the UI updates after generation.
Hooks handle unauthenticated/unauthorized errors consistently with existing patterns.

file_operations.description: Add NOC hooks: useGetAIStatus, useGetTreasuryStatus, useGetDaoProposals, useVoteOnProposal, useGetZkCurrentRoot, useGenerateZkSolvencyProof, useGetOmniMeshStatus, and useStartUniversalLaunch (mapping to the backend universal launch method) using the existing useActor hook and React Query patterns (enabled gating on actor/isFetching, queryKey naming, and invalidation for proposals/root/state after mutations).

requirements.summary: Add an admin-only NOC Admin section on the NOC page for Principal-based user add/remove and universal launch/black-hole mode toggle, visible only when backend reports the caller is admin.
acceptanceCriteria: When isAdmin is true, the NOC Admin section is visible; otherwise it is hidden.
Admin can add/remove a Principal and see user count update (or a success message).
Universal launch / black-hole toggle controls are disabled/hidden for non-admin users.

file_operations.description: Create an admin-only NOC Admin UI section component with: (1) Principal text input + Add/Remove actions, and (2) universal launch/black-hole mode toggle controls; use React Query mutation hooks and show success/error feedback in English; ensure non-admin users never see the section.

Wire the NOC Admin section into the NOC page, rendering it only when the backend-derived admin check (React Query hook calling the actor) indicates the caller is admin; ensure the universal launch toggle controls are not rendered for non-admin users.

Add admin-focused NOC hooks required by the NOC Admin section (e.g., useNocAddUserByPrincipal, useNocRemoveUserByPrincipal, useNocGetUserCount, and useNocIsAdmin-style checks), aligning with existing queryKey/invalidation patterns; implement invalidation so user count/related admin UI refreshes after add/remove.

requirements.summary: Port NOC UI components (ComplianceShield, InstitutionalConsole, UniversalLaunchOverlay) into the React+Tailwind app and update any unsupported dependencies to use actor + React Query hooks.
acceptanceCriteria: Components compile and render inside the current React app.
UniversalLaunchOverlay reads omni-mesh status via backend actor calls (not hardcoded REST fetch).
UniversalLaunchOverlay triggers a backend method for “startUniversalLaunch” (or equivalent) and updates UI state accordingly.

file_operations.description: Port/create the ComplianceShield component as a React component styled with the existing Tailwind setup and shadcn/ui primitives where appropriate; ensure it integrates cleanly into the existing app component patterns. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Port/create the InstitutionalConsole component as a React component; replace any prior REST/proxy assumptions with props and/or React Query hook data supplied by the NOC page. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Update/complete the UniversalLaunchOverlay to remove any unsupported dependencies (e.g., getNOCActor, fetch('/api/...')) and instead use the existing actor + React Query hooks for omni-mesh status and universal launch actions/state; ensure UI updates after actions via query invalidation/refetch. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Render the ported NOC components (ComplianceShield, InstitutionalConsole, UniversalLaunchOverlay) within the NOC dashboard page and connect them to the appropriate hook-driven data/actions so there are no orphan components.

requirements.summary: Add navigation entry (desktop + mobile) to reach /kd-platform/noc while keeping existing routes intact and without modifying immutable paths.
acceptanceCriteria: A user can navigate to the NOC page from the main navigation on desktop and mobile.
Existing navigation items and KD Platform routes continue to function.

file_operations.description: Add a new navigation item (desktop + mobile) that routes to '/kd-platform/noc' and update active-state logic so KD Platform highlighting includes the new NOC route, without changing any existing route paths.

requirements.summary: Update project documentation to describe ICP-native (dfx-based) deployment and verification for NOC methods, without relying on Vercel/Node proxy routes.
acceptanceCriteria: README includes dfx-based deploy and verification commands for the NOC methods.
No requirement depends on Vercel-proxied /api/noc/* endpoints to function.
The documented verification commands correspond to actual backend methods implemented in this build.

file_operations.description: Add/update a frontend-scoped README section documenting ICP-native (dfx-based) deployment for the frontend + single canister, and how to verify NOC capabilities using 'dfx canister call' against the candid-exposed methods (avoid any Vercel-proxy /api/noc/* instructions).
